title: Rim
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the Rim|the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]], originally known as the Rim}}
{{Quote|See, that's the problem with the Republic. In the Core everything's great: people are healthy, wealthy, and happy. But out here on the Rim things aren't so easy.|[[Darth Bane/Legends|Dessel]]|<ref name="DB:PoD">''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''</ref>}}
[[File:TheRim22kBBY-TEA.jpg|thumb|right|275px|The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] "Core" and the Rim around 22,000 BBY]]
The '''Rim''' was a colloquial term, somehow pejorative, used to designate the most rimward regions of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], namely the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]], the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] and the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The concept of Rim was commonly used in opposition to the [[Greater Core]], best known simply as the Core, another colloquial term that designated the most coreward regions of the galaxy, including the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] and the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Although the distinction between the Core and the Rim had never been formally recognized, it had large cultural and political implications<ref name="TEA" /> throughout galactic history.<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|The worlds of the Core had joined together to form the Republic. But there were dangers beyond the Rim—the old empire of Xim, the slave kingdoms of the Hutts, and all manner of satrapies and dominions that your historians have forgotten.|[[Arden Lyn]]|<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>}}
The distinction between the Core and the Rim originated the most ancient times of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], when the Republic space encompassed an area similar to the Greater Core while the rest of the galaxy was already known as the Rim.<ref name="TEA" /> Hence, inhabitants of the Core often considered themselves superior and more civilized than the [[Rimmer/Legends|Rimmers]].<ref name="DB:PoD" /><ref name="Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref> The earliest incarnation of the Rim, during the [[Expansionist Period/Legends|Expansionist Era]], was in fact equivalent to the modern Inner Rim, that was later included in the Greater Core. Intensive colonization of the Rim began during the [[Great Manifest Period/Legends|Great Manifest Period]].<ref name="TEA" /> The contrast between the safe Core and the dangerous Rim, dominated by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] and the [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]], pushed many Rim worlds to join the Republic and accelerated its galactic expansion.<ref name="TEGtW" />

Millennia later, during the [[Pius Dea Crusades]] (c. [[11,000 BBY]]), the Rim was ravaged by the Republic controlled by the [[Pius Dea]] sect.<ref name="TEGtW" /> Following the crusades, the tensions between the Core and the Rim persisted throughout galactic history.<ref name="TNEC" /> Furthermore, the Rim would be widely neglected by galactic governments.<ref name="TEGtW" /> At the beginning of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], the Rim was attacked by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] without causing any response from the Republic. During the [[New Sith Wars]], the Republic had lost control of the Rim to the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Sith]] and separatist forces.<ref name="TEGtW" /> At that time, the galactic government was not even able to maintain the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] communications network to the Rim.<ref name="TEA" />

In [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], following the end of the New Sith Wars, the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] dissolved [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Armed Forces]] to promote the development of [[Planetary Security Forces|local sector forces]]. The reform was less popular in the Rim since poor remote Rim sectors were unable to compensate Republic forces by their own. In the last centuries of the Republic, the authority of the Galactic Republic declined progressively in the Rim. In [[124 BBY]], the Republic declared the entire [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and parts of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] a free-trade zone in order to foster economy and rebuild ties between the Rim and the Core. For many reasons, the project was a failure.<ref name="TEGtW" /> Later, the taxation of Rim trade routes prompted separatism.<ref name="TEA" /> By the final decades of the Republic, large portions of the Rim were abandoned to the Hutts or exploited by corrupt [[Senator/Legends|senators]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> At that time, the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] was divided between the [[Human/Legends|Human]]-dominated [[Core Faction/Legends|Core Faction]] and the [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]]-supportive [[Rim Faction/Legends|Rim Faction]].<ref name="CoD">''[[Cloak of Deception]]''</ref> During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Statute 312b|Fourth Constitutional Amendment]] gave more voting power to planets of the Core Worlds, Colonies, and Inner Rim, prompting the secession of Rim worlds into the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="TEA" /> Near the end of the Clone Wars, the Separatists reinforced their positions within the Rim territories.<ref name="TNEC" /> After the war, the newly-formed [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] struggled to [[Reconquest of the Rim|reconquer]] the [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]] strongholds in the Rim.<ref name="TEGtW" />

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], the Empire prohibited [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] in the Rim.<ref name="TEA" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] was more supported in the Rim than in the Core. Immediately after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], countless worlds joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], most of them in the Rim territories.<ref name="TNEC" />

Decades later, during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic defense forces]] were blamed for abandoning the Rim to the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="TEGtW" />
